title: ceraia tetraedris
intro: Ceraia tetraedris, also known as the "Three-sided Ceraia", is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its unique three-sided leaf structure. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Ceraia tetraedris include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make decoctions or infusions, which are consumed as teas or used topically in the form of creams or ointments.
